pinterest.text: Most 6-year-olds take 4 tries. Some crack it on the first. Every wrong turn is a micro-decision. They don't know they're problem-solving. That's the point. Save this for Saturday morning. More puzzles like this in JoyMaze — free on iOS & Android.

instagram.text:
"Hand this to your kid. Don't say a word. See what happens. 
Eyes narrow. Pencil slows. Then — the sound of it stopping. They got it. 
That frustration before the breakthrough — that's the skill. Not the finished maze. 
How fast did yours solve it? Drop the time below. 
More like this in JoyMaze — link in bio."

youtube.text:
Watch what happens when a 6-year-old gets handed a maze with no instructions. They scan, they backtrack, they try again. That's spatial reasoning — and they don't know it. For parents who choose activities with intention. More printable puzzles at joymaze.com.
